title: Avionics Technicians
description: Install, inspect, test, adjust, or repair avionics equipment, such as radar, radio, navigation, and missile control systems in aircraft or space vehicles.

tasks.text: Test and troubleshoot instruments, components, and assemblies, using circuit testers, oscilloscopes, or voltmeters.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Keep records of maintenance and repair work.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Adjust, repair, or replace malfunctioning components or assemblies, using hand tools or soldering irons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install electrical and electronic components, assemblies, and systems in aircraft, using hand tools, power tools, or soldering irons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up and operate ground support and test equipment to perform functional flight tests of electrical and electronic systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assemble components such as switches, electrical controls, and junction boxes, using hand tools or soldering irons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lay out installation of aircraft assemblies and systems, following documentation such as blueprints, manuals, and wiring diagrams.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Connect components to assemblies such as radio systems, instruments, magnetos, inverters, and in-flight refueling systems, using hand tools and soldering irons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Interpret flight test data to diagnose malfunctions and systemic performance problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Coordinate work with that of engineers, technicians, and other aircraft maintenance personnel.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Fabricate parts and test aids as required.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assemble prototypes or models of circuits, instruments, and systems for use in testing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate computer-aided drafting and design applications to design avionics system modifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
